name: Iznajar and its Reservoir via the Colada de Valdearenas
name_original: Iznájar y su Embalse por la Colada de Valdearenas
region_intro: Jogging routes around Iznájar offer diverse terrain, from the expansive shoreline of the Iznájar Reservoir to the rugged landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. The region features rolling hills covered in olive groves, providing undulating paths for varied runs. Additionally, the charming village streets of Iznájar itself offer unique urban jogging experiences. Best jogging routes around Iznájar The most popular jogging route is Iznajar and its Reservoir via the Colada de Valdearenas, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic water views along the vast reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Cerrillo de la Buitrera Loop from Rute, a difficult 4.1 miles (6.5 km) path. This route winds through a rugged mountain environment with significant elevation gain. Local runners also love the Ascent to El Canuto loop from Rute, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail leading through the challenging terrain of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Jogging around Iznájar is defined by its vast reservoir, rolling olive groves, and mountain trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Iznájar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Iznájar?
A: There are over 15 dedicated jogging routes around Iznájar listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences. These include 10 moderate trails and 5 more challenging options for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging trails in Iznájar?
A: While many routes are moderate, the expansive shoreline of the Iznájar Reservoir offers extensive flat and undulating paths perfect for easier runs. These provide scenic water views without significant elevation changes, ideal for beginners or those seeking a relaxed pace.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging routes in the area?
A: Yes, routes along the Iznájar Reservoir, particularly around Valdearenas Beach, are generally suitable for families. These areas offer relatively flat terrain and beautiful scenery, making them enjoyable for all ages. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails around Iznájar?
A: Many of the natural trails and country roads through the olive groves and along the reservoir are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001004009/iznajar-and-its-reservoir-via-the-colada-de-valdearenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznajar and its Reservoir via the Colada de Valdearenas&lt;/a&gt; route is a great option for a scenic run with your canine companion.

Q: Are there circular jogging routes in Iznájar?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Iznájar are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1288090761/cerrillo-de-la-buitrera-loop-from-rute" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrillo de la Buitrera Loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging circular path through rugged mountain terrain.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I incorporate into my running routes?
A: Iznájar is rich with viewpoints offering breathtaking vistas. Consider routes that pass by Mirador del Postigo for views of the reservoir and olive groves, or Mirador Cruz de San Pedro for spectacular lake and village views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1853196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cedrón Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of three Andalusian provinces.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the jogging routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can encounter historical sites. The medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/884528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop the village, offers a historical landmark and exceptional views. For those seeking a challenge, routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Tiñosa&lt;/a&gt;, the highest peak in Córdoba province, provide stunning mountain scenery.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in Iznájar?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Iznájar village and at popular starting points for trails, especially near the reservoir and Valdearenas Beach. For routes starting further afield, such as those near Rute, specific parking areas are usually indicated.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Iznájar?
A: Spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging in Iznájar, with mild weather and lush landscapes. Winter can also be suitable, especially on sunny days, but summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs preferable.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Iznájar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from the tranquil reservoir paths to the challenging mountain trails, and the stunning natural beauty of the olive groves and Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. The Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park offers rugged mountain environments with significant elevation gain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Canuto loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.3-mile trail with substantial climbing, provide a demanding workout for experienced runners.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer views of the Iznájar Reservoir?
A: Many routes in the region feature spectacular views of the Iznájar Reservoir, often referred to as the 'Lake of Andalusia'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001004009/iznajar-and-its-reservoir-via-the-colada-de-valdearenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznajar and its Reservoir via the Colada de Valdearenas&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering continuous scenic water views along its 4.2-mile path.

Q: Are there running routes that pass through the famous olive groves of Andalusia?
A: Yes, the region surrounding Iznájar is characterized by endless olive groves and rolling hills. Numerous trails and country roads wind through these picturesque landscapes, providing undulating routes where you can immerse yourself in the quintessential Andalusian agricultural scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rute – Rute loop from Rute&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate run through such terrain.
tips: Fed by the Genil River, this is the largest reservoir in Andalusia. It was built between 1959 and 1969 and in 2010 work was carried out to improve and modernize the dam. It has an Interpretation Center where some of the tools used in its construction are exhibited, which had its complexities due to the high temperatures.
The Arbabian Castillo is located at the highest point of Iznajar, along with the Parroquial de Santiago Apostol.

waypoints.name: Iznájar Castle and the Parish Church of Saint James the Apostle
